text: Laparoscopic proctocolectomy with an iliopouch anal anastomosis represents the latest development in the evolution of surgical treatment of UCMFAP. We present the case of a thirty-seven year old female with an eight year history of chronic ulcerative colitis which was refractory to medical therapy.

The patient is positioned in the Lloyd Davis position with the surgical team arranged around her. Two monitors are set up for optimal visualization. A 45 cm parambulical incision is made for the blunt port insertion, and pneumoperitoneum is established. Two 5 mm ports are placed in the left lower quadrant and suprapubic midline. A disc of skin and subcutaneous fat is excised from the stoma site in the right lower quadrant. The patient is then placed in a moderately steep Trendelenburg position with the left side elevated. The left lateral peritoneal reflection is opened alongside the distal sigmoid colon to identify and protect the left ureter. The sigmoid and descending colon are mobilized medially in a cephalad direction by dissecting along the white line of Toldt.
The surgeon carefully avoids mobilizing the kidney by maintaining dissection within the medial aspect of the paracolic gutter.

The surgeon mobilizes the splenic flexure by performing medial dissection of the omentum from the distal transverse colon and lateral dissection of the colon from the retroperitoneum. This reveals the body and tail of the pancreas and the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV). The patient is repositioned in Trendelenburg with the right side up, and the small bowel is moved out of the pelvis. The right ureter is identified and protected. The cecum is retracted in a cephalad and medial direction, and the peritoneum around the base of the cecum and terminal ileum is scored to enter the retroperitoneal plane.

The surgeon develops a plane medially to the inferior vena cava (IVC) and duodenum, opening the right lateral peritoneal reflection. The ascending colon is mobilized medially towards the duodenum, and the hepatic flexure is mobilized below the level of the umbilicus. The entire right colon is moved to the midline, exposing and protecting the IVC and duodenum. The hepatic flexure is further mobilized off the retroperitoneal structures and medially to the Gerota's fascia, completing the medial extent of its mobilization with duodenum exposure.

The surgeon focuses on the pelvis, continuing the dissection distally along the left parorectal peritoneum. The precircular space is entered at the sacral promontory, and dissection proceeds distally within this space.

The surgeon scores the right parorectal peritoneum, ensuring the right ureter is protected. Dissection is joined in the pre-sacral space, achieving full circumferential dissection of the rectum in the extramasorectal bloodless plane down to the pelvic floor. A sponge stick is placed in the vagina to identify the rectovaginal septum, crucial for avoiding injury in patients with a rectocele. The rectum is dissected down to the anal canal and transected using two firings of a laparoscopic linear articulated 45mm stapler.

The pneumoperitoneum is evacuated, and the entire colon is exteriorized from the terminal ileum to the transected edge of the rectum through the parumbilical incision. The mesentery is divided and ligated with double ligatures on the larger vessels. The ileocolic pedicle and omentum are preserved. The terminal ileum is transected with a linear stapler approximately four centimeters from the ileocecal valve, and the specimen is removed. The entire small bowel is exteriorized and examined to exclude Crohn's disease. The small bowel, except for the terminal ileum used for the pouch, is returned to the abdominal cavity with correct orientation.

A 15-centimeter J-pouch is constructed and returned to the abdominal cavity, ensuring correct orientation of the pouch mesentery. The anus is gently dilated, and an EEA stapler is inserted with the spike brought out adjacent to the staple line. The pouch is positioned into the pelvis, and the anvil is docked into the stapler, which is then fired and removed. A large round Jackson-Pratt drain is placed through the suprapubic port site. A loop of distal ileum above the pouch is exteriorized to the premarked stoma site in the right lower quadrant, where the ileostomy is matured with interrupted sutures.
